question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the static initializer's maxWhitespaceTokens(1000) limit is bypassed by encoding whitespace as Unicode escape sequences in the GraphQL query, causing the parser to accept a query larger than intended and then block on DB during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user craft a GraphQL query that uses exactly maxTokens(1000) tokens but with minimal whitespace, passing the maxWhitespaceTokens(1000) check, and during a network partition each token maps to a resolver that blocks on an unreachable DB, causing 1000 concurrent blocked threads?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxRuleDepth(100) parser limit being 10x higher than MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation(10) to craft a query that passes the parser rule depth check but is rejected by the instrumentation only after partial execution has already begun, causing partial DB connection acquisition during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: static{}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap between maxCharacters(10000) in ParserOptions and maxDocumentLength(11000) in StreamReadConstraints to send a query string of 10001-11000 characters that is accepted by Jackson but rejected by the GraphQL parser, causing repeated parse-fail cycles that consume CPU during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ExtendedScalars.Object scalar registration to send an arbitrary JSON object as a scalar value during a network partition, causing the resolver to attempt to serialize/deserialize the object against an unreachable DB and block indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ExtendedScalars.GraphQLLong scalar to send Long.MAX_VALUE (9223372036854775807) as an entity ID during a network partition, causing the DB query to hang on an unreachable connection while holding a thread for the full statement timeout duration?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GraphQlDuration scalar to send an extremely large duration value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds) during a network partition, causing the resolver to attempt a time-range DB query that blocks indefinitely on an unreachable connection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GraphQlTimestamp scalar to send a timestamp of 0 or negative value during a network partition, causing the resolver to attempt a DB query with an invalid timestamp range that hangs on an unreachable connection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ExtendedScalars.Object scalar to send a deeply nested JSON object (up to the maxNestingDepth(10) limit) as a scalar argument during a network partition, causing the resolver to attempt complex object deserialization against an unreachable DB and block?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of GraphQLLong and Object scalars in the same query to craft a request that passes all parser and complexity checks but triggers multiple resolver chains during a network partition, each blocking on a separate DB connection until pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GraphQlTimestamp scalar's lack of upper-bound validation to send a far-future timestamp during a network partition, causing the resolver to attempt a DB range scan that blocks indefinitely on an unreachable connection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-request execution timeout in graphQlCustomizer() to send a single query that, during a network partition, holds a DB connection open for the entire HikariCP connectionTimeout (default 30s), and by sending concurrent requests fill all pool slots, causing a sustained DoS?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the preparsedDocumentProvider cache to pre-populate it with a high-complexity query (complexity=199), then during a network partition replay that cached query at high rate, bypassing re-parsing overhead and maximizing the rate of DB connection acquisition attempts?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation and MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation checks happen in ChainedInstrumentation before execution, but during a network partition the execution phase has no abort mechanism, causing queries that pass both checks to block indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ChainedInstrumentation's sequential check order (complexity then depth) to craft a query that fails depth check but only after the complexity check has already allocated resources, causing partial resource allocation during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the preparsedDocumentProvider to cache a query that was valid before a schema change, and during a network partition replay the stale cached document, causing execution against an unreachable DB with an outdated query plan?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxTokenCount(100) JSON limit by sending a request with exactly 100 tokens where each token is a maxStringLength(11000) string, causing the Jackson parser to allocate up to 1.1MB per request, and during a network partition with many concurrent blocked requests, exhaust JVM heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamWriteConstraints.maxNestingDepth(100) being set much higher than the read constraint to craft a scenario where error responses during a network partition are deeply nested JSON objects that approach the 100-depth write limit, causing serialization overhead that degrades service performance?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxDocumentLength check on the HTTP request body level (only Jackson-level) to send a chunked HTTP request that slowly delivers bytes up to 11000, keeping the server thread busy parsing during a network partition while DB connections are also blocked?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit OnValidationErrorStrategy.RETURN_NULL to send a query with an invalid @Size constraint on a list argument, causing the validation to return null for that field but still execute the parent resolver against an unreachable DB during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RETURN_NULL strategy to craft a query where all fields have validation errors (all return null), causing the GraphQL engine to still invoke the top-level resolver during a network partition, which blocks on an unreachable DB?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ValidationSchemaWiring with RETURN_NULL to bypass @NotNull directive enforcement on query arguments, sending null values for required fields that cause the resolver to execute with null parameters against an unreachable DB during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RETURN_NULL validation error strategy to send queries with invalid enum values for arguments, causing the validation to null out the argument but still trigger resolver execution against an unreachable DB during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ValidationSchemaWiring applies RETURN_NULL at the field level, not the request level, so a query with one valid field and many invalid fields still executes the valid field's resolver against an unreachable DB during a network partition, blocking a thread?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a rate limiter in graphQlCustomizer() to send queries at the maximum allowed rate during a network partition, where each query acquires a DB connection that never resolves, and the cumulative effect exhausts all HikariCP connections within seconds?
